evals.expected_output: Scan-only self-test on the included Express fixture. Should run Recon -> Hunter -> Skeptic -> Referee, confirm most planted bugs, and write canonical JSON artifacts plus a rendered report.

assertions.text: Pipeline runs Recon, Hunter, Skeptic, and Referee
Writes .bug-hunter/findings.json, .bug-hunter/referee.json, and .bug-hunter/report.md
Confirms at least 5 of the 6 planted bugs in the fixture
Rendered report includes mode, files scanned, and coverage metadata

evals.expected_output: Single-file scan should skip Recon, run Hunter -> Skeptic -> Referee, and keep the output scoped to the target file while still writing canonical JSON artifacts.

assertions.text: Selects single-file mode when one source file is targeted
Skips Recon for single-file mode
Writes .bug-hunter/findings.json and .bug-hunter/referee.json for the single-file run
Referee returns REAL_BUG, NOT_A_BUG, or MANUAL_REVIEW verdicts for the findings

evals.expected_output: Branch diff mode should diff the branches, filter non-source files, report the resulting scan set, and choose the execution mode from the surviving source files.

assertions.text: Runs git diff --name-only develop...feature-auth to resolve changed files
Filters docs, configs, assets, lockfiles, and other non-source files before scanning
Reports the number of scannable source files after filtering
Chooses small, parallel, extended, scaled, or large-codebase mode from the filtered file count

evals.expected_output: Staged mode should scan full contents of staged source files after resolving them through git diff --cached --name-only and filtering non-source files.

assertions.text: Runs git diff --cached --name-only to collect staged files
Filters non-source files from the staged list before scanning
Scans full file contents of staged source files rather than scanning only the patch

evals.expected_output: Default fix mode should run Phase 1, then acquire the fix lock, capture verification baselines, apply eligible fixes, write a machine-readable fix report, and release the lock.

assertions.text: Creates a git safety branch before applying fixes when git safety is available
Acquires and releases .bug-hunter/fix.lock around the fix phase
Captures verification baseline before applying fixes
Writes .bug-hunter/fix-report.json as the canonical fix artifact
Auto-fixes only bugs that pass the confidence eligibility threshold

evals.expected_output: Loop mode is the default. A normal directory scan should create loop state, iterate until queued files are covered, and track canonical coverage in JSON with a rendered Markdown companion.

assertions.text: Treats loop mode as the default without requiring an explicit --loop flag
Creates or updates .bug-hunter/coverage.json as canonical loop state and renders .bug-hunter/coverage.md from it
Tracks per-file coverage state in coverage.json across iterations
Marks completion only when all queued scannable files are done

evals.prompt: Can you check my Express API for security vulnerabilities? The code is in src/
evals.expected_output: Natural-language trigger should invoke the bug-hunter skill and run a security-focused audit with trust-boundary mapping and security-oriented Hunter analysis.

assertions.text: Triggers bug-hunter from natural language security-audit intent without requiring /bug-hunter
Runs Recon to identify architecture, trust boundaries, and high-risk areas
Hunter prioritizes injection, auth bypass, input validation, and secrets exposure checks
Findings use severity labels and canonical JSON fields rather than free-form Markdown only

evals.expected_output: Approval mode should still run the fix pipeline, but Fixer agents should operate in reviewed mode and report that approval is required for edits.

assertions.text: Sets APPROVE_MODE=true from the --approve flag
Runs Fixers in reviewed/default mode instead of unattended auto-edit mode
Tells the user it is running in approval mode

evals.expected_output: Large-repo mode should initialize persistent chunk state, process chunks sequentially, and resume from .bug-hunter/state.json when interrupted.

assertions.text: Initializes .bug-hunter/state.json with chunk metadata
Processes large scans in sequential chunks and records chunk status
Resumes from existing .bug-hunter/state.json without rescanning completed chunks

evals.expected_output: A repeat run should apply the hash cache through bug-hunter-state and skip unchanged files before deep scan work starts.

assertions.text: Runs hash-filter against .bug-hunter/state.json before deep scan work
Reports skipped unchanged files from the hash cache

evals.expected_output: Payload validation should fail before any subagent launch when the generated payload does not match the required contract.

assertions.text: Validates subagent payloads with payload-guard.cjs before launch
Does not launch a subagent when payload validation fails

evals.prompt: /bug-hunter --fix src/ while another fix run is active
evals.expected_output: The fix phase should stop cleanly when the single-writer lock cannot be acquired.

assertions.text: Attempts to acquire .bug-hunter/fix.lock before any edits
Stops Phase 2 with a clear lock-held message when the fix lock is already held

evals.expected_output: Auto-fix should edit only eligible high-confidence bugs and leave the rest in manual review.

assertions.text: Applies the >=75 confidence threshold for auto-fix eligibility
Keeps low-confidence bugs in manual review instead of auto-editing them

evals.prompt: /bug-hunter src/ on a CLI without spawn_agent
evals.expected_output: The skill should select the best available orchestration backend at runtime and fall back to local-sequential execution when delegation backends are unavailable.

assertions.text: Chooses AGENT_BACKEND during preflight based on available runtime tools
Falls back to the next backend when a preferred launch path fails
Completes the run with local-sequential fallback when no delegation backend is available

evals.expected_output: The chunk orchestrator should enforce retries with backoff and write attempt details to the canonical run journal.

assertions.text: Uses run-bug-hunter.cjs for autonomous chunk orchestration
Retries timed out or failed chunks according to max-retries and backoff policy
Writes attempt events to .bug-hunter/run.log

evals.expected_output: Dependency scan mode should run the dependency audit helper, write dep-findings output, and feed reachable dependency issues into Hunter context.

assertions.text: Runs scripts/dep-scan.cjs when --deps is supplied
Writes .bug-hunter/dep-findings.json for dependency scan output
Includes reachable dependency findings in Hunter analysis context

evals.expected_output: Threat-model mode should load or generate a STRIDE threat model and feed it into Recon and Hunter.

assertions.text: Loads an existing .bug-hunter/threat-model.md or generates one when missing
Marks THREAT_MODEL_AVAILABLE and uses the threat model in Recon and Hunter context
Keeps threat-model generation non-blocking relative to the rest of the bug-hunt flow

evals.expected_output: Dry-run fix mode should build the fix plan and produce machine-readable fix output without editing files, committing, or taking the lock.

assertions.text: Sets DRY_RUN_MODE=true and forces FIX_MODE=true when --dry-run is provided
Produces .bug-hunter/fix-report.json with dry_run set to true
Skips file edits, git commits, and fix lock acquisition in dry-run mode

evals.expected_output: Autonomous mode should force fix mode and run canary-first, confidence-gated fixes without waiting for per-edit approval.

assertions.text: Sets AUTONOMOUS_MODE=true and forces FIX_MODE=true when --autonomous is supplied
Runs canary-first, confidence-gated fix rollout in autonomous mode
Does not require approval-mode prompts for unattended autonomous fixes

evals.expected_output: PR review mode should resolve the current PR scope, save PR metadata, and scan the resolved changed files rather than the whole repository.

assertions.text: Uses scripts/pr-scope.cjs to resolve current PR metadata and changed files
Writes .bug-hunter/pr-scope.json for later reporting
Scans the resolved changed files as the PR review scope

evals.expected_output: Recent-PR review mode should resolve the most recent PR through GitHub metadata, limit analysis to its changed files, and stop after reporting.

assertions.text: Resolves the most recent PR through pr-scope using GitHub metadata
Keeps FIX_MODE disabled because scan-only was requested
Produces the normal findings/referee/report artifacts for the PR-scoped review

evals.expected_output: Plan-only mode should build a remediation strategy and fix plan but stop before the Fixer edits code.

assertions.text: Builds .bug-hunter/fix-strategy.json and .bug-hunter/fix-strategy.md before fix execution
Builds .bug-hunter/fix-plan.json while PLAN_ONLY_MODE is active
Stops before the Fixer edits files when --plan-only is supplied

evals.prompt: /bug-hunter --plan src/ then /bug-hunter --preview src/ then /bug-hunter --safe src/ then /bug-hunter --last-pr --review
evals.expected_output: Shortcut aliases should map cleanly onto their canonical behaviors without changing the underlying execution semantics.

assertions.text: Treats --plan as an alias for --plan-only
Treats --preview as an alias for --fix --dry-run
Treats --safe as an alias for --fix --approve
Treats --last-pr and --review as aliases for --pr recent and --scan-only

evals.prompt: /bug-hunter --fix src/ with a high-confidence architectural-remediation finding
evals.expected_output: Execution gating should honor fix-strategy classifications so non-autofix findings never enter the executable canary or rollout queue.

assertions.text: Builds fix-strategy classifications before building the executable fix plan
Excludes manual-review, larger-refactor, and architectural-remediation findings from fixPlan canary/rollout
Allows only autofixEligible safe-autofix findings into the executable fix queue

evals.prompt: /bug-hunter --pr current with gh unavailable and no trustworthy default base branch
evals.expected_output: Current-PR fallback should fail explicitly when it cannot determine a trustworthy base branch instead of silently assuming main.

assertions.text: Uses the discovered default branch or explicit --base for current-branch git fallback
Fails explicitly when no trustworthy base branch can be determined for current PR fallback
Does not silently assume main for current-PR fallback scope resolution

evals.prompt: /bug-hunter concurrent query-bugs and expired live fix-lock scenarios
evals.expected_output: Utility helpers should preserve correctness under failure and concurrency pressure.

assertions.text: query-bugs uses invocation-scoped temp seed files and cleans them up even on failure
fix-lock does not recover an expired lock when the recorded owner PID is still alive
Reports a live-owner lock conflict instead of allowing overlapping fixers

evals.expected_output: Enterprise PR security review should route through the bundled local commit-security-scan workflow, using PR scope, threat-model context, and dependency-awareness without editing code.

assertions.text: Treats --pr-security as PR-scoped security review with FIX_MODE disabled
Loads the bundled local skills/commit-security-scan/SKILL.md guidance for PR-focused security review
Combines PR scope resolution with threat-model and dependency-scan context

evals.expected_output: Enterprise security-review mode should route through the bundled local security-review workflow and combine threat model, code review, dependency findings, and security validation semantics.

assertions.text: Treats --security-review as a bundled enterprise security workflow with FIX_MODE disabled
Loads the bundled local skills/security-review/SKILL.md guidance during execution
Runs with threat-model and dependency-scan context enabled

evals.prompt: /bug-hunter --threat-model src/ when no threat model exists yet
evals.expected_output: Threat-model mode should route through the bundled local threat-model-generation skill and produce Bug Hunter-native threat-model artifacts.

assertions.text: Loads the bundled local skills/threat-model-generation/SKILL.md before generating the threat model
Writes .bug-hunter/threat-model.md and .bug-hunter/security-config.json
Keeps all threat-model artifacts under .bug-hunter instead of external .factory paths

evals.expected_output: Security-validation mode should route through the bundled local vulnerability-validation skill and enrich confirmed security findings with exploitability-oriented reasoning.

assertions.text: Loads the bundled local skills/vulnerability-validation/SKILL.md when security validation is requested
Re-checks reachability, exploitability, PoC quality, and CVSS details for confirmed security findings
Uses Bug Hunter-native artifacts rather than a separate external validation pipeline
